tool.name: rcx-tk correct MSDial alignments
tool.description: Groups duplicate alignments in MSDial output files by aggregating metadata and abundances.
tool.command: #if $input_file.ext == "tabular" #set $input_file_new = 'input.tsv' #else #set $input_file_new = 'input.' + str($input_file.ext) #end if cp ${input_file} ${input_file_new} && python3 -m rcx_tk --method msdial ${input_file_new} 'output.tsv'

param.name: input_file
param.label: MSDial alignment result file
param.help: Upload your MSDial alignment result file.

tool.help:
.. _rcx_tk_msdial:
=============================== rcx-tk: MSDial Alignment Corrector ===============================
**Tool Description**
This tool processes MSDial alignment result files to identify and group duplicate alignments. Duplicate alignments (rows sharing identical abundance values in any sample column) are merged into single representative entries. Metadata columns are concatenated and abundance values are aggregated using the maximum value across the duplicates.
How it works ------------
- **Duplicate detection**: Identifies alignment rows that share the same non-zero abundance value in any sample column. - **Cluster merging**: Transitively merges overlapping duplicate groups into clusters. - **Aggregation**: For each cluster, averages the first three metadata columns, concatenates the remaining metadata, and takes the maximum abundance across samples.
Inputs ------
- **MSDial alignment result file**: The alignment result table exported from MSDial software. Supported formats: CSV, TSV, tabular, XLS, XLSX.
Outputs -------
- **Corrected alignments**: A TSV file with duplicate alignments merged and non-duplicate alignments unchanged.
Notes -----
- The input file is expected to follow the standard MSDial alignment result format with 3 header rows followed by the column header row. - The first 28 columns after the Alignment ID are treated as metadata; all subsequent columns are treated as sample abundance values.
